text:
There was once a woman who didn't think much of cats. She found them meowing and climbing into her bedsheets irritating. So when a gray tabby kitty drifted into her home, she scoffed.

"Out! Go chase some birds," she snapped.

But he stayed. That night, as she lay wide awake, staring at her dark ceiling, the cat paced beside her bed. Feline patience isn't my strength, she realized.

Next morning, the gray feline lounged on her feet. She sighed.

"Thanks for company," she conceded.

He nuzzled her hand.

"It's hard to stay away," he murmured.

Days turned into weeks—she couldn't help cuddling him.

Once she decided to play with him. She threw a toy. He caught it. She tossed a treat. He fetched it effortlessly.

She smiled.

"I guess I could get used to this."

He miaowed contentedly.

"Hey, maybe we could get a pet?"
